# UX Lite setup for non-technical designers

Goal: keep the workflow simple, visual and close to a chat + preview experience.

## One-time setup

1. Open Extensions in VS Code.
2. Install the recommended extension: Live Preview (ms-vscode.live-server).
3. Reload VS Code if prompted.

## Daily workflow

1. Open an HTML prototype in the workspace.
2. Run command: Live Preview: Show Preview (Internal Browser).
3. Split editor side by side:
   - left: Chat
   - center: HTML file
   - right: Preview
4. Edit in chat or file, preview reloads automatically after save.

## Notes

- Workspace is configured to auto-save after short delay.
- Most technical visual noise is reduced (minimap, breadcrumbs, sticky scroll, extra markers).
- Terminal opens in editor area when needed, not in bottom panel.

## Optional UX profile (manual)

For an even cleaner look, users can manually hide views they do not need:

1. View -> Appearance -> Hide Secondary Side Bar.
2. Keep only Explorer and Chat icons in Activity Bar.
3. Keep one tab visible at a time.
